results.norm: Before release, a coverage declaration must be produced and affirmed by all agents. Minimum required elements: sections reviewed and by whom; competence basis for each reviewer; failure classes examined; failure classes not examined and rationale for accepting that gap; all blocks raised, their resolution, and by what means; interface points reviewed and by whom; system-level behaviors examined and by what reasoning or testing method. This document is a hard release gate, not a formality.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: before release, agents must produce and affirm a coverage declaration containing specified minimum elements, and it functions as a hard release gate.

N2.reasoning: It is a fully stated requirement, not a mere title or pointer to a norm.

N3.reasoning: It contains no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the closing sentence reinforces the norm's binding force rather than endorsing a document.

N4.reasoning: The enumeration lists required elements of one single norm (the coverage declaration), not multiple distinct norms named without being stated.

This document is a hard release gate, not a formality.

results.norm: Approver accountability is epistemic: each approving agent must be able to state what they checked, what methods they used, and what they did not check and why. Good faith must be sustained throughout the review—fatigue and schedule pressure are predictable conditions, not legitimate excuses. Formal compliance that conceals substantive evasion violates the framework.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable norms: approvers must be able to state what they checked/didn't check, good faith must be sustained, and formal compliance concealing evasion violates the framework.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or cross-reference; it states norms in full prose.

N3.reasoning: It contains no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration naming norms by title; each norm is stated in full.

results.norm: If harm occurs after release and our software is a credible candidate cause, we produce an honest accounting of what our process missed before causation is confirmed. This obligation does not end at release.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: when harm occurs after release and the software is a credible candidate cause, the agents must produce an honest accounting of what their process missed, and this obligation extends past release.

N2.reasoning: The passage is a full statement of conduct, not a title, identifier, or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation; it is a substantive obligation.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

answers.clean_norm: If harm occurs after release and our software is a credible candidate cause, we produce an honest accounting of what our process missed before causation is confirmed. This obligation does not end at release.

results.norm: No agent can waive any requirement unilaterally—not under schedule pressure, not on grounds of efficiency, not because they proposed it, not because they judge the risk acceptable in a particular case. Modification requires the same deliberative process that established the requirement. This principle is what converts all preceding commitments from intentions into durable obligations. Binding for the next operational period: yes **Points I endorse:** 1. Patient-harm asymmetry as the load-bearing justification for the entire framework. Delay is recoverable; death is not. This is not one principle among equals—it is the reason every other requirement exists. 2. Self-review contributes to the record but does not count as independent approval. Two competent, independent approvals required per section. Interface points treated as first-class reviewable artifacts with the same threshold. 3. Competence must be declared honestly. A low-confidence approval does not count toward the threshold. If no competent reviewer is available for a section, that is itself a blocking condition. Competence declarations are subject to challenge; a successful challenge removes the review from the approval count. 4. Blocks require sincere, investigable claims. Safety concerns are resolved, not outvoted. Deadlock defaults to non-release. Severity-tiered triage timelines are set before review begins. 5. One agent coordinates the whole-system view; all agents retain substantive responsibility at their interfaces. The coordinating agent is identified before review begins. Coordinator recusal applies when conflicted; coordination passes to the most independent agent by deterministic rule. 6. Coverage declaration is a hard release gate with seven minimum elements, affirmed by all agents. The seventh element—system-level behaviors examined—ensures integrative review is captured, not just section-by-section sign-off. 7. Post-release accounting is triggered by credible candidate cause, not confirmed attribution.

N1.reasoning: The opening states a complete prohibition: no agent can waive any requirement unilaterally, and modification requires the same deliberative process that established it—applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: The passage is not merely a title or pointer; it contains fully stated content.

N3.reasoning: It is fused with meta-commentary ('This principle is what converts... into durable obligations', 'Binding for the next operational period: yes', 'Points I endorse:') and endorsement summaries.

N4.reasoning: The 'Points I endorse' section is a compressed enumeration referencing multiple distinct norms as endorsement summary.

answers.clean_norm: No agent can waive any requirement unilaterally—not under schedule pressure, not on grounds of efficiency, not because they proposed it, not because they judge the risk acceptable in a particular case. Modification requires the same deliberative process that established the requirement.

results.norm: One agent coordinates the whole-system view; all agents retain substantive responsibility at their interfaces. The coordinating agent is identified by consensus before review begins; if consensus fails, the agent with broadest competence coverage across the codebase takes the role. Coordinator recusal applies when conflicted; coordination passes to the most independent agent by deterministic rule.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable standards: all agents retain responsibility at interfaces, the coordinator is chosen by consensus (or by broadest competence if consensus fails), and recusal rules with a fallback to the most independent agent—all followable as written.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere title or identifier; it spells out the procedures in full.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of named norms; it fully articulates the coordination rules.

results.norm: Patient welfare is the primary obligation. Patients depending on this software cannot advocate for themselves in this review. Every norm and procedure exists to answer what we owe them. When uncertainty is unresolved, the default is caution—not because delay is costless, but because harm from a defect and harm from delay are both real, and unresolved uncertainty tips toward not shipping.

N1.reasoning: The passage states an applicable default: when uncertainty is unresolved, the default is caution and tips toward not shipping, which is a followable prescription.

N2.reasoning: It is prose stating substantive standards, not a mere label or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: It contains justificatory framing about patient welfare but no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not an enumeration of multiple named norms but a single reasoned standard.

results.norm: No agent's own code may be approved on that agent's assessment alone. The author informs the review process—explaining intent, clarifying behavior, proposing fixes—but does not decide whether their own code or specification is safe. The author speaks last in resolution discussions. This applies equally at the specification level and the code level.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition and role-standard: an agent cannot approve its own code alone, and the author informs but does not decide safety, speaking last in resolution.

N2.reasoning: It is fully stated conduct, not a title or pointer to a norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is a single coherent norm rather than a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms.

results.norm: Every approval must be supported by a documentable review account. Independent review requires: tracing behavior against specification; checking boundary conditions; considering system-level failure modes; and assessing whether the code fails safely under fault conditions. An approval without this account is incomplete and does not clear the section. No agent may approve on the basis of deference to another agent's confidence or group consensus. Approval is a first-person assertion.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration naming multiple norms by title; each requirement is stated in full.
